selected_recipes.title: Scrumptious Sauteed Tofu
selected_recipes.ingredients: 1 lb Firm tofu|2 c Chicken or vegetable broth|1 Clove garlic; minced and pressed|1/2 c Soy sauce|Lemon-pepper seasoning to taste|1 tb Cajun hot-pepper seasoning (more if you like hot; but remember; it is also pretty salty)|1/2 c Cornstarch|Cooking oil or spray-on cooking oil
selected_recipes.instructions: Slice the tofu into 1/2 inch slices. Pat dry and place in a deep bowl. Combine the broth ( you can use hot water and powdered broth mix for this) and the next three ingredients and pour over the tofu. Let sit in the refrigerator at least 8 hours. Remove tofu slices from the marinade and pat dry. Combine the cornstarch and hot-pepper seasoning. and shake over the sliced tofu. Heat oil in a skillet and sauti the slices until they are light brown and crisp. Serve immediately. Serves two as main dish or four as appetizer. If serving as an appetizer, cut tofu into 1 inch cubes. It is also delicious saut'ed without the cornstarch crust. (And a little less caloric, although it is already pretty low in fat.) This is delicious with a side of sesame noodles and steamed or stir-fried oriental vegetables. It also makes a nice "chicken" salad or a sandwich. The marinade makes a nice soup base. You can also purchase the Cajun seasoning in a salt-free version. **Even my "I hate tofu" friend ate this up and asked for seconds. Just don't tell anyone it's tofu til after they stuff themselves. (N. C. KENFIELD)

selected_recipes.title: Fresh Tomato Pizzas
selected_recipes.ingredients: 8 Melba rounds or toasts|2 sm Plum tomatoes; thinly sliced|2 tb Chopped red onion|2 tb Shredded part-skim mozzarella cheese|1 pn Dried oregano
selected_recipes.instructions: 1. Top each melba toast with tomato, onion, cheese and oregano. Arrange prepared melbas on microwaveable dish. 2. Microwave on High until cheese melts, about 1 to 1 1/2 minutes. (To bake in conventional oven, preheat oven to 400°F. Bake prepared snacks on cookie sheets until cheese has melted, about 5 minutes.) Makes 8 servings. Microwave time: About 1 to 1 1/2 minutes. Conventional oven time: About 5 minutes. Per serving: About 19 cal, 1 g pro, 3 g car, 0 g fat, 19% cal from fat, 1 mg cholesterol, 28 mg sod, 0 g fiber.

selected_recipes.title: Merguez And Dried Currant Couscous Salad
selected_recipes.ingredients: 1 1/2 c Water|1 Box couscous; (about 1 1/2 cups) (10-ounce)|6 Merguez sausages|3/4 c Dried currants|1/4 c Store bought lemon herb dressing
selected_recipes.instructions: Bring water to a boil. Remove from heat. Stir in couscous, cover, and let sit for 10 minutes. Meanwhile, brown the sausages in a pan. Fluff the couscous with a fork, making sure to break apart any lumps. Stir in the currants and dressing. Top with the merguez. Yield: 4 servings Per serving: 956 Calories (kcal); 1g Total Fat; (1% calories from fat); 26g Protein; 214g Carbohydrate; 0mg Cholesterol; 37mg Sodium Food Exchanges: 9 Grain(Starch); 0 Lean Meat; 0 Vegetable; 5 1/2 Fruit; 0 Fat; 0 Other Carbohydrates
